---
title: "ubuntu 12.04 has experienced an internal error"
date: 2013-11-03
forum: New to Ubuntu
---

### Post by swipisnt on 2013-11-03
hello guys. i know this is not first post about this problem, but cant find solution to fix this.
ubuntu 12.04 has experienced an internal error. and this is few times a day. this is somethink with compiz? now its sais something wrong with unity panel service, the same happens and telling me error on compiz. not sure what i can see in file xsession-error but some warnings and fails in there. here the xsession-error file hope some one can help.[ATTACH]247542[/ATTACH]

---

### Post by deadflowr on 2013-11-03
Does it ask you to report the error?
Generally the crash reports are written by the program apport.
The report is in the folder /var/crash.

If nothing seems wrong, or the problems are not a big deal, you can disable apport by opening up the file
/etc/default/apport
and changing the line
enabled=1
to
enabled=0
This will have to be done with root rights.
so either sudo nano, or gksu gedit should do.

Personally though, I feel reporting the problem at least once, if possible, is best.

---

### Post by swipisnt on 2013-11-03
yes it ask what to do report crash or whatever in details was unix panel service and at the end something about compiz. disable not solve the problem. you see im new in ubuntu so i want to know this is big deal or not and how (if possible) to solve this problem :)

---

### Post by deadflowr on 2013-11-03
Is the system unusable?

That's the gauge to measure if it's a problem or not.

You could post the output of the crash report.

---
